description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a rounded bowl and an attached cistern at the back. The toilet is predominantly white but shows significant signs of wear, with visible stains and discoloration on the bowl, seat, and base. The seat and lid are both raised, revealing the interior of the bowl, which appears to be ceramic and is heavily soiled. The cistern is rectangular and sits directly above the bowl, with no visible flush mechanism on its exterior. The base of the toilet is broad and flat, designed to be mounted securely to the floor. The overall appearance suggests it is an older, utilitarian fixture commonly found in public or urban environments.
description: A worn, white ceramic toilet with a raised seat and lid, attached cistern, and visible stains throughout. The design is utilitarian and typical of public or urban restroom facilities.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat and lid, stained bowl interior, and the full face of the cistern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, seat, and cistern, with visible grime along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the plumbing connection, with minimal detail on the bowl.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the side of the bowl, seat, and cistern, with visible dirt and wear.
